name: User Interaction Pattern Cluster
hypothesis: Telegram/communication channel response latency patterns produce meaningful gradients for idle vs active detection
description: Captures communication channel health metrics (response time, uptime, activity patterns) to create a user interaction cluster in GRAO. This cluster distinguishes between idle system state and active user engagement, producing gradients that steer toward self-initiation when idle.
lessons_learned: Communication channel health traces produce meaningful idle vs active detection patterns
Response latency metrics create distinct cluster from system maintenance experiences
Channel diversity (Telegram, Discord, Signal, WhatsApp, Nostr) increases cluster richness

customization.how_to_add:
To add your own user interaction cluster:
1. Set env vars for your communication channels (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, etc.)
2. Configure collect functions in trace-collector.js to check your channel's health
3. Record response latency, uptime, activity patterns
4. Add to GRAO experience store with this schema
5. Track idle vs active ratio to produce self-initiation gradients
customization.generic_pattern: Each channel health check produces a trace with response_time, success, uptime metrics. These traces cluster together in GRAO as 'user interaction' patterns, distinct from 'system maintenance' patterns.
